text:
Once upon a time, there was a curious turtle who wanted to discover the secrets behind the moonlit flowers that only bloomed under the silver moonlight.

One moonlit night, the turtle slipped out of its cozy shell for the first time in a long while. It scurried across the meadow, stopping only occasionally to listen to the birds' songs.

The first bloomed flower was beautiful, radiating with silver moonlight.

It snapped open wide to reveal a silvery moth that flitted gracefully from flower to flower.

The turtle admired the moth's beauty, then gently lifted its own tiny nose to admire the flower's shine.

The moth fluttered out from the flower and floated gracefully through the air.

"Thank you," whispered the turtle.

The moth fluttered gently back to the flower.

"Welcome back!"

The turtle gently pet the flower's petals.

"Well," the moth fluttered again, "I've been listening to the songs of the evening songsters."

"You sing?"
